sections.heading: This Prudential Standard is made under:
sections.title: Prudential Standard authority basis
sections.summary: This section specifies that the Prudential Standard is enacted under three specific sections of Australian legislation: the Banking Act 1959, Insurance Act 1973, and Life Insurance Act 1995. It establishes the legal foundation for the standard by referencing relevant regulatory acts that authorize its implementation.

requirements.text: This Prudential Standard is made under section 11AF of the Banking Act 1959
This Prudential Standard is made under section 32 of the Insurance Act 1973
This Prudential Standard is made under section 230A of the Life Insurance Act 1995

sections.source_quote: This Prudential Standard is made under: (a) section 11AF of the Banking Act 1959 (Banking Act); (b) section 32 of the Insurance Act 1973 (Insurance Act); and (c) section 230A of the Life Insurance Act 1995 (Life Insurance Act).

sections.heading: This Prudential Standard applies to all ‘APRA-regulated institutions',1 defined
sections.title: APRA-regulated institutions scope
sections.summary: This section defines which financial institutions fall under the Prudential Standard. It includes authorized deposit-taking institutions (ADIs), foreign ADIs, non-operating holding companies, general insurers, Category C insurers, authorized insurance NOHCs, parent entities of Level 2 insurance groups, life companies, friendly societies, eligible foreign life insurance companies (EFLICs), and registered life NOHCs. The scope covers all APRA-regulated entities in Australia and internationally.

requirements.text: This Prudential Standard applies to all 'APRA-regulated institutions' defined as: all authorised deposit-taking institutions (ADIs), including foreign ADIs, and non-operating holding companies authorised under the Banking Act

This Prudential Standard applies to all 'APRA-regulated institutions' defined as: all general insurers, including Category C insurers, non-operating holding companies authorised under the Insurance Act, and parent entities of Level 2 insurance groups

This Prudential Standard applies to all 'APRA-regulated institutions' defined as: all life companies, including friendly societies and eligible foreign life insurance companies (EFLICs), and non-operating holding companies registered under the Life Insurance Act

sections.source_quote: This Prudential Standard applies to all 'APRA-regulated institutions', defined as: (a) all authorised deposit-taking institutions (ADIs), including foreign ADIs, and non-operating holding companies authorised under the Banking Act (authorised banking NOHCs); (b) all general insurers, including Category C insurers, non-operating holding companies authorised under the Insurance Act (authorised insurance NOHCs) and parent entities of Level 2 insurance groups; and (c) all life companies, including friendly societies and eligible foreign life insurance companies (EFLICs), and non-operating holding companies registered under the Life Insurance Act (registered life NOHCs).
sections.relevance_reason: Defines scope of APRA-regulated institutions for data compliance

sections.heading: Terms that are defined in Prudential Standard 3PS 001 Definitions, Prudential
sections.title: Defined terms reference
sections.summary: This section states that terms defined in other Prudential Standards (APS 001, GPS 001, LPS 001) will be highlighted in bold when first used in this standard. It ensures consistency in terminology across regulatory documents by directing users to the relevant definitions.

requirements.text: Terms that are defined in Prudential Standard 3PS 001 Definitions, Prudential Standard APS 001 Definitions (APS 001), Prudential Standard GPS 001 Definitions (GPS 001) or Prudential Standard LPS 001 Definitions appear in bold the first time they are used in this Prudential Standard

sections.source_quote: Terms that are defined in Prudential Standard 3PS 001 Definitions, Prudential Standard APS 001 Definitions (APS 001), Prudential Standard GPS 001 Definitions (GPS 001) or Prudential Standard LPS 001 Definitions appear in bold the first time they are used in this Prudential Standard.

sections.heading: For the purposes of this Prudential Standard:
sections.title: Group and Head definitions
sections.summary: This section defines key terms for group structures within APRA-regulated institutions. It specifies what constitutes a 'group', 'Head of a group', 'Level 2 group', and 'Level 2 Head' based on regulatory frameworks. These definitions are critical for understanding organizational hierarchies and reporting responsibilities under business continuity management.

requirements.text: For the purposes of this Prudential Standard, 'group' means a Level 2 group or a Level 3 group, as relevant
For the purposes of this Prudential Standard, 'Head of a group' means a Level 2 Head or Level 3 Head, as relevant
For the purposes of this Prudential Standard, 'Level 2 group' means the entities that comprise: Level 2 as defined in APS 001 or a Level 2 insurance group as defined in GPS 001

For the purposes of this Prudential Standard, 'Level 2 Head' means: where an ADI that is a member of a Level 2 group is not a subsidiary of an authorised banking NOHC or another ADI, that ADI; where an ADI that is a member of a Level 2 group is a subsidiary of an authorised banking NOHC, that authorised banking NOHC; or the parent entity of a Level 2 insurance group as defined in GPS 001

sections.source_quote: For the purposes of this Prudential Standard: 'group' means a Level 2 group or a Level 3 group, as relevant; 'Head of a group' means a Level 2 Head or Level 3 Head, as relevant; 'Level 2 group' means the entities that comprise: (a) Level 2 as defined in APS 001; or (b) a Level 2 insurance group as defined in GPS 001; 'Level 2 Head' means: (a) where an ADI that is a member of a Level 2 group is not a subsidiary of an authorised banking NOHC or another ADI, that ADI; (b) where an ADI that is a member of a Level 2 group is a subsidiary of an authorised banking NOHC, that authorised banking NOHC; or (c) the parent entity of a Level 2 insurance group as defined in GPS 001.

sections.heading: The Head of a group must notify APRA in accordance with paragraph 36 if the
sections.title: Major disruption notification
sections.summary: This section mandates that the Head of a group must notify APRA if an institution experiences a major disruption that could materially impact its risk profile or financial soundness. The notification is required unless another APRA-regulated institution within the group has already reported the same information to APRA.

requirements.text: The Head of a group must notify APRA in accordance with paragraph 36 if the institution experiences a major disruption that has the potential to have a material impact on the institution's risk profile, or affect its financial soundness

The Head of a group must notify APRA in accordance with paragraph 36 if the institution experiences a major disruption that has the potential to have a material impact on the institution's risk profile, or affect its financial soundness, except where an APRA-regulated institution within the group has otherwise notified APRA of that information

sections.source_quote: The Head of a group must notify APRA in accordance with paragraph 36 if the institution experiences a major disruption that has the potential to have a material impact on the institution's risk profile, or affect its financial soundness, except where an APRA-regulated institution within the group has otherwise notified APRA of that information.
sections.relevance_reason: APRA incident reporting for financial stability

sections.heading: An APRA-regulated institution must identify, assess, manage, mitigate and report
sections.title: Business continuity risk management
sections.summary: This section requires APRA-regulated institutions to identify, assess, manage, mitigate, and report on potential business continuity risks. The purpose is to ensure institutions can meet financial and service obligations to depositors, policyholders, and other stakeholders during disruptions.

requirements.text: An APRA-regulated institution must identify, assess, manage, mitigate and report on potential business continuity risks
An APRA-regulated institution must ensure it is able to meet its financial and service obligations to its depositors, policyholders and other stakeholders

sections.source_quote: An APRA-regulated institution must identify, assess, manage, mitigate and report on potential business continuity risks to ensure that the institution is able to meet its financial and service obligations to its depositors, policyholders and other stakeholders.
sections.relevance_reason: Tangential: general risk management not specific to data

sections.heading: The Board must ensure that the business continuity risks and controls are taken
sections.title: Business continuity risks integrated into risk management strategy
sections.summary: The Board must ensure business continuity risks and controls are incorporated into the institution's overall risk management strategy and included in the risk management declaration submitted to APRA. This requirement applies to APRA-regulated financial institutions to maintain alignment with regulatory expectations for risk management.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates that business continuity risks be systematically integrated into risk management, which is critical for assessing cross-border data transfer impacts in financial institutions due to the need for comprehensive risk coverage.

requirements.text: The Board must ensure business continuity risks and controls are taken into account as part of the institution's risk management strategy
The Board must ensure business continuity risks and controls are included in the risk management declaration required to be provided to APRA

sections.source_quote: The Board must ensure that the business continuity risks and controls are taken into account as part of the institution's risk management strategy and when completing a risk management declaration required to be provided to APRA.
sections.relevance_reason: APRA risk management for financial institutions

sections.heading: BCM is a whole-of-business approach that includes policies, standards and
sections.title: Business continuity as whole-of-business approach
sections.summary: Business continuity management (BCM) requires institutions to establish policies, standards, and procedures that ensure critical business operations can be maintained or recovered during disruptions. This approach aims to minimize financial, legal, regulatory, and reputational consequences from operational interruptions.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines BCM as a whole-of-business approach, which is relevant for DTIAs as it establishes foundational requirements for maintaining critical operations during disruptions, impacting data transfer resilience.

requirements.text: BCM includes policies, standards and procedures for ensuring critical business operations can be maintained or recovered in a timely fashion during disruptions
BCM aims to minimise financial, legal, regulatory and reputational consequences arising from disruptions

sections.source_quote: BCM is a whole-of-business approach that includes policies, standards and procedures for ensuring that critical business operations can be maintained or recovered in a timely fashion, in the event of a disruption. Its purpose is to minimise the financial, legal, regulatory, reputational and other material consequences arising from a disruption.
sections.relevance_reason: Supporting: BCM policies for financial data continuity

sections.heading: When conducting the BIA, the APRA-regulated institution must consider:
sections.title: Business impact analysis considerations for disruptions
sections.summary: APRA-regulated institutions must evaluate plausible disruption scenarios, operational downtime periods, impact on depositors/policyholders, and financial/legal/regulatory consequences when conducting a Business Impact Analysis (BIA). This ensures comprehensive risk assessment for critical operations.
sections.dtia_summary: This section specifies key considerations for BIAs, which are essential for DTIAs to evaluate the impact of disruptions on data transfers, particularly regarding financial and regulatory consequences.

requirements.text: The institution must consider plausible disruption scenarios over varying periods of time
The institution must determine the period of time for which critical business operations could not operate without the institution
The institution must assess the extent to which disruptions might impact depositors and/or policyholders
The institution must evaluate financial, legal, regulatory and reputational impacts of disruptions over varying periods

sections.source_quote: When conducting the BIA, the APRA-regulated institution must consider: (a) plausible disruption scenarios over varying periods of time; (b) the period of time for which the institution could not operate without each of its critical business operations; (c) the extent to which a disruption to the critical business operations might have a material impact on the interests of depositors and/or policyholders of the institution; and (d) the financial, legal, regulatory and reputational impact of a disruption to the institution's critical business operations over varying periods of time.

sections.heading: An APRA-regulated institution must notify APRA as soon as possible and no
sections.title: Notification of major disruptions to APRA
sections.summary: APRA-regulated institutions must notify APRA within 24 hours of experiencing a major disruption that could materially impact their risk profile or financial soundness. The notification must detail the disruption's nature, actions taken, expected effects, and timeline for recovery, with a follow-up notification upon resumption of normal operations.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires timely reporting of disruptions to APRA, which is critical for DTIAs to ensure data transfer continuity during incidents and to maintain regulatory oversight of cross-border data flows.

requirements.text: APRA-regulated institutions must notify APRA as soon as possible and no later than 24 hours after experiencing a major disruption
The institution must explain the nature of the disruption, actions being taken, likely effects, and timeframe for returning to normal operations
The institution must notify APRA when normal operations resume

sections.source_quote: An APRA-regulated institution must notify APRA as soon as possible and no later than 24 hours after the institution experiences a major disruption that has the potential to have a material impact on the institution's risk profile, or affect its financial soundness.

sections.heading: APRA may adjust or exclude a specific requirement in this Prudential Standard
sections.title: APRA's authority to adjust prudential standards
sections.summary: APRA has the authority to adjust or exclude specific requirements within this Prudential Standard for regulated institutions, subject to relevant legal provisions under the Banking Act, Insurance Act, and Life Insurance Act.
sections.dtia_summary: This section grants APRA flexibility to modify requirements, which may impact DTIAs by allowing tailored approaches to data transfer compliance in specific regulatory contexts.

requirements.text: APRA may adjust or exclude a specific requirement in this Prudential Standard in relation to an APRA-regulated institution

sections.source_quote: APRA may adjust or exclude a specific requirement in this Prudential Standard in relation to an APRA-regulated institution.
sections.relevance_reason: APRA's authority to adjust standards for financial institutions
